content.full_text:
February 7, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

To whom it may concern:

I am a private pilot and own my own airplane. A situation has arisen where I might be of assistance to a candidate for state office, by flying him around to various speaking engagements.

My intention and expectation is that I would be reimbursed, by the candidate’s campaign committee, for a pro rata share of direct operating costs (gas, oil, parking or tiedown fees, landing fees), and not for any indirect costs such as hangar, insurance, maintenance, etc. I should note that under Federal Aviation Regulation 61.118, it would be in violation of Federal law if I took any greater reimbursement than I have described here.

"Pro rata" would be defined, for purposes of such reimbursement, as the proportionate share of such direct operating costs based on the number of people in the aircraft. For instance, if the candidate and one staff person were flown by me, they would reimburse me for 2/3 of such direct costs (2 of them, and 3 people total in the plane). This also is in conformance with the requirements of the cited FAR.

Is the procedure I have described fully in compliance with state law related to political contributions, candidates for public office, etc.?

Since this situation is far more likely to be covered either by regulation or by statutory interpretation, rather than by any literal reading of a statute, I need to turn to you for further information. Thanks for your help.

Sincerely,

Larry Israel

P.S. Candidates for federal office are treated differently. This is covered in FAR 91.59. It would be useful if the State had a similar or identical provision, but I’ve found no indication that’s the case. Even there were such a condition, FAR 91.59 would first have to be modified to permit State action.
